figures.caption: Fig. 6: Pose-tracking state estimation on the RealCupboardOpenAndPlace task. Left: successful articulation with accurate pose tracking. Right: failure case where insufficient pose-tracking precision causes the policy to stall or oscillate during articulation.
TABLE IV: Real-world evaluation over 25 trials per task. Articulation success is counted only in trials where grasping succeeded. Time to success is averaged over successful trials.
TABLE VI: Real-world ablation comparing pose-tracking and marker-based state estimation on the drawer and cupboard tasks. Time to success averaged over successful trials.

TABLE V: Simulation ablations comparing WHOLE-MoMa with architectural and design variants. Success rates over 50 episodes with 95% confidence intervals. Time to success averaged over successful trials.

Fig. 2: A whole-body controller (WBC) provides a strong but sub-optimal motion-generation prior: it solves a multi-objective optimization problem for the various embodiments in question, minx wee∥eee∥2 +wbase∥ebase∥2 +wposture∥eposture∥2, under constraints such as self-collision and joint limits, but optimizing the current objective does not guarantee a good future interaction state. Here, w and e denote task weights and tracking errors, respectively.

TABLE III: Simulation results for WHOLE-MoMa and all baselines. Success rates are evaluated over 50 episodes per task, reported with 95% confidence intervals. Time to success is averaged over successful trials.

Fig. 3: WHOLE-MoMa pipeline. Parameter-randomized WBC rollouts produce whole-body demonstrations scored with a reward rt combining task success, articulation joint angle change ∆qart, and end-effector or base distance reduction (∆dee or ∆dbase, depending on the task). Transitions are grouped into horizon-H chunks with discounted return Rchunk(t). An implicit Q-chunked transformer Qθ(st,at:t+H−1) is learned via chunked TD error and used to compute the advantage A(st,at:t+H−1). A Diffusion Policy πϕ is then trained with the chunk-level AWR objective Lchunk
